report_name: Kyber Ransomware Debuts with Post-Quantum Encryption, Targeting Windows and ESXi

threat_actors.name: Kyber
threat_actors.description: A new ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation that emerged in March 2026, targeting both Windows and VMware ESXi environments. The group is notable for using the Kyber1024 post-quantum cryptography algorithm in its Windows variant, likely as a psychological tactic to pressure victims into paying the ransom.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through data encryption and the threat of data leakage.

attack_patterns.name: System Information Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The Windows variant of the Kyber ransomware gathers system information as part of its attack chain.

attack_patterns.name: Service Stop
attack_patterns.description: The Kyber ransomware's Windows variant terminates SQL and Exchange services to ensure files are unlocked for encryption.

attack_patterns.name: Clear Windows Event Logs
attack_patterns.description: The Windows variant of Kyber ransomware wipes Windows Event Logs to cover its tracks and impede forensic analysis.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The Windows variant of Kyber ransomware deletes volume shadow copies and disables boot repair to prevent system recovery.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Kyber ransomware encrypts files on Windows systems and virtual machine files (.vmdk, .vmsn, .vswp) on VMware ESXi hosts to disrupt operations.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The Kyber ransomware operation employs a double extortion model, which implies that data is exfiltrated from victim networks before encryption.

attack_patterns.name: Internal Defacement
attack_patterns.description: The ESXi variant of Kyber ransomware defaces the ESXi host's login page (index.html) with a ransom message.

malware.name: Kyber Ransomware (Windows variant)
malware.description: A Rust-based ransomware variant targeting Windows systems. It uses AES-CTR for file encryption and protects the symmetric key with a hybrid scheme of Kyber1024 (a post-quantum algorithm) and X25519.

malware.name: Kyber Ransomware (ESXi variant)
malware.description: A Linux-based ransomware variant targeting VMware ESXi environments. It encrypts virtual machine files using the ChaCha8 cipher and RSA-4096 for key protection, and defaces the ESXi management interface.

identities.name: U.S. defense contractor
identities.description: A multi-billion-dollar American defense contractor that was listed as an early victim on the Kyber ransomware gang's data leak site.

infrastructure.name: Kyber Ransom Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The command and control (C2) and data leak site infrastructure used by the Kyber ransomware operation for extortion.

indicators.name: Kyber Ransomware VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Kyber Ransomware VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b9a1c8d-4e6f-4b1a-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware TTP to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/kyber-ransomware-gang-toys-with-post-quantum-encryption-on-windows/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-24
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, though rare in automated scripts.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the command-line pattern used by Kyber ransomware to delete volume shadow copies using vssadmin.exe, hindering system recovery.

indicators.name: Kyber Ransomware Wevtutil Event Log Clearing
indicators.pattern:
title: Kyber Ransomware Wevtutil Event Log Clearing
id: 8d3c1b9a-6f4e-4a1b-9d8c-f2a3b4c5d6e7
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of wevtutil.exe to clear Windows Event Logs, a common ransomware TTP for defense evasion.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/kyber-ransomware-gang-toys-with-post-quantum-encryption-on-windows/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-24
tags:
  - attack.t1070.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wevtutil.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' cl '
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, though clearing logs is highly suspicious.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the command-line pattern used by Kyber ransomware to clear Windows Event Logs using wevtutil.exe to hide its activity.

indicators.name: Kyber Ransomware ESXi VM Termination
indicators.pattern:
title: Kyber Ransomware ESXi VM Termination
id: c1b9a8d3-4e6f-4b1a-8c9d-e1f2a3b4c5d6
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of esxcli to terminate running virtual machines, a precursor to encryption by ESXi-targeting ransomware like Kyber.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/kyber-ransomware-gang-toys-with-post-quantum-encryption-on-windows/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-24
tags:
  - attack.t1489
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/esxcli'
      - 'esxcli'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'vm'
      - 'process'
      - 'kill'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative action to forcefully shut down a VM.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects the use of the `esxcli vm process kill` command on VMware ESXi hosts, which is used by the Kyber ransomware to terminate virtual machines before encryption.

relationships.source_name: Kyber Ransomware VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
Kyber Ransomware Wevtutil Event Log Clearing
